mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing periods do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the sign is negative in more than 55% of easing observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent levels indicates weakening household income and demand, which lowers expected corporate sales and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the average 20d return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST captures weakening consumer demand and housing activity, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60d SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where both series are falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger real output and industrial momentum, which improves earnings breadth and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upturns are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from random.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness rises and multiples contract, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FedFunds inflation episodes do not produce lower 20d SPX returns than low-rate episodes, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals job losses and weaker aggregate demand, which depresses expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average 5d return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An inventory-like demand shock is reflected when GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS weakens, indicating broad consumption and output deceleration that lowers equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail sales weakness does not predict lower 60d SPX returns than either series alone, or if the signal reverses sign in expansionary windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST combined with stable or falling UNRATE indicates reacceleration in real activity and household formation, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturns with stable unemployment do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined condition fails to beat an unconditional long-only benchmark.
